sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
I see:
- Player at rows 32-35, cols 36-38 (N)
- Center frame still at [[32, 32], [32, 35], [35, 35], [35, 32]] (c) - NOT broken!
- Left frame still at [[32, 24], [32, 27], [35, 27], [35, 24]] (c) - still charcoal
- White frame at [[32, 39], [32, 43], [35, 43], [35, 39]] (W) - this is to the RIGHT of the player
- Blue target at [[33, 41], [33, 42], [34, 42], [34, 41]] (b) - inside white frame
The center frame is still there. The player is at cols 36-38, to the RIGHT of the center frame (cols 32-35). I need to move LEFT to overlap the center frame.
LEFT moves -3 cols: 36-38 → 33-35. At cols 33-35, the player overlaps with the center frame (cols 32-35). That should break it.
But wait - the white frame is at cols 39-43. If I move LEFT, the player goes to cols 33-35. The center frame is at cols 32-35. The player would be at cols 33-35, overlapping with cols 32-35. That should work.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Center frame still intact at cols 32-35
# Player at cols 36-38, need to move LEFT to overlap frame
# LEFT moves -3 cols: 36-38 -> 33-35, overlaps with center frame cols 32-35

action(['LEFT'])
</parameter>
